Lots has changed over the years, past thirty years I lived on LOES.
The neighbourhood is the same, the crowd is younger and less hip than the 'good ol'days' when it was not safe to walk the street of alphabet city ( Tompkins Square or below 14 Street) As the saying goes, everybody living here had an FBI dossier or on the run from Feds...
The area changed ethnicities, Polish-Ukrainian (Slavic) and Jewish the turn of 20th century, to lawless in 1970's, becoming hip in 1980's, with inevitable gentrification and flood of Japanese/ Korean businesses in 1990's due to NYU campus nearby. Now, the bland "nulties" brought the influx of aimless LGBT gawkers, gay and misfit tourism from Europe and afar.
Does not feel authentic, fake and pointless, overpriced... with cost of 1 BR walk up apartment make my eyes bleed.
No authenticity, just overpriced coffee places charging $7 USD for double skinny latte  with soy milk.
It was okay.  Good view from the 11th floor. Complementary breakfast was okay.  Need a non pork alternative for protein offering.
